channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-11650] sanity-sec test 17 fails with 'test_17 returned 1': syntax error: operand expected (error token is "[0]")
item.description:
sanity-sec test_17 fails with no obvious failure and with no error message. For the failure at https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/feedbaca-e388-11e8-bfe1-52540065bddc , the only obvous issue is the following in the client test_log

/usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity-sec.sh: line 1273: [0]: syntax error: operand expected (error token is "[0]")

Looking at sanity-sec.sh, we see this error takes place in the do_fops_quota_test() routine

1272         qused_new=$(nodemap_check_quota "$run_u")
1273         [ $((qused_new)) -lt $((qused_low)) \
1274                 -o $((qused_new)) -gt $((qused_high)) ] &&
1275                 error "quota not reclaimed, expect $qused_orig, " \
1276                       "got $qused_new, fuzz $quota_fuzz"
item.summary: sanity-sec test 17 fails with 'test_17 returned 1': syntax error: operand expected (error token is "[0]")

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
+1 on b_es6_0

https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/99dbd27f-40af-4a8d-a06c-197245bdabf2

It fails because $(nodemap_check_quota "$run_u") gets an error from "lfs quota -q".

[ 8615.959143] LustreError: 376322:0:(osc_quota.c:319:osc_quotactl()) ptlrpc_queue_wait failed, rc: -5

 Something wrong with the OST0000:

[ 8615.965952] LustreError: 11-0: lustre-OST0000-osc-ffff932805446800: operation ost_connect to node 10.240.22.208@tcp failed: rc = -114
[ 8621.081506] LustreError: 11-0: lustre-OST0000-osc-ffff932805446800: operation ost_connect to node 10.240.22.208@tcp failed: rc = -114
[ 8626.201346] LustreError: 11-0: lustre-OST0000-osc-ffff932805446800: operation ost_connect to node 10.240.22.208@tcp failed: rc = -114
[ 8631.321053] LustreError: 11-0: lustre-OST0000-osc-ffff932805446800: operation ost_connect to node 10.240.22.208@tcp failed: rc = -114

At the same time at OST side:

[ 8643.913740] Lustre: ll_ost00_011: service thread pid 378681 was inactive for 40.663 seconds. The thread might be hung, or it might only be slow and will resume later. Dumping the stack trace for debugging purposes:
[ 8643.917054] Pid: 378681, comm: ll_ost00_011 4.18.0-477.27.1.el8_lustre.ddn17.x86_64 #1 SMP Fri Nov 15 09:56:14 UTC 2024
[ 8643.918915] Call Trace TBD:
[ 8643.919655] [<0>] do_get_write_access+0x1e0/0x410 [jbd2]
[ 8643.920666] [<0>] jbd2_journal_get_write_access+0x5b/0x80 [jbd2]
[ 8643.921745] [<0>] __ldiskfs_journal_get_write_access+0x36/0x80 [ldiskfs]
[ 8643.923058] [<0>] osd_write+0x350/0xcd0 [osd_ldiskfs]
[ 8643.924079] [<0>] dt_record_write+0x32/0x120 [obdclass]
[ 8643.925289] [<0>] tgt_client_data_update+0x660/0x9f0 [ptlrpc]
[ 8643.926787] [<0>] tgt_client_del+0x576/0x750 [ptlrpc]
[ 8643.927795] [<0>] ofd_obd_disconnect+0x1e5/0x200 [ofd]
[ 8643.928802] [<0>] target_handle_disconnect+0x213/0x470 [ptlrpc]
[ 8643.929933] [<0>] tgt_disconnect+0x4a/0x180 [ptlrpc]
[ 8643.930918] [<0>] tgt_request_handle+0xc9c/0x1a80 [ptlrpc]
[ 8643.931984] [<0>] ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x323/0xbd0 [ptlrpc]
[ 8643.933193] [<0>] ptlrpc_main+0xbff/0x1510 [ptlrpc]
[ 8643.934157] [<0>] kthread+0x134/0x150
[ 8643.934891] [<0>] ret_from_fork+0x35/0x40
[ 8643.935657] Pid: 365787, comm: ll_ost00_006 4.18.0-477.27.1.el8_lustre.ddn17.x86_64 #1 SMP Fri Nov 15 09:56:14 UTC 2024
[ 8643.937535] Call Trace TBD:
[ 8643.938096] [<0>] do_get_write_access+0x29b/0x410 [jbd2]
[ 8643.939085] [<0>] jbd2_journal_get_write_access+0x5b/0x80 [jbd2]
[ 8643.940183] [<0>] __ldiskfs_journal_get_write_access+0x36/0x80 [ldiskfs]
[ 8643.941386] [<0>] osd_write+0x350/0xcd0 [osd_ldiskfs]
[ 8643.942324] [<0>] dt_record_write+0x32/0x120 [obdclass]
[ 8643.943314] [<0>] tgt_client_data_update+0x660/0x9f0 [ptlrpc]
[ 8643.944447] [<0>] tgt_client_del+0x576/0x750 [ptlrpc]
[ 8643.945442] [<0>] ofd_obd_disconnect+0x1e5/0x200 [ofd]
[ 8643.946385] [<0>] target_handle_disconnect+0x213/0x470 [ptlrpc]
[ 8643.947503] [<0>] tgt_disconnect+0x4a/0x180 [ptlrpc]
[ 8643.948492] [<0>] tgt_request_handle+0xc9c/0x1a80 [ptlrpc]
[ 8643.949566] [<0>] ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x323/0xbd0 [ptlrpc]
[ 8643.950771] [<0>] ptlrpc_main+0xbff/0x1510 [ptlrpc]
[ 8643.951735] [<0>] kthread+0x134/0x150
[ 8643.952419] [<0>] ret_from_fork+0x35/0x40
[ 8646.255395] Lustre: lustre-OST0000: Export 00000000324dad83 already connecting from 10.240.24.111@tcp

sanity-sec test 18 is also hitting this issue on master (2.13.0):
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/f978df96-b567-11e9-a1bd-52540065bddc

sanity-sec test 19 hit the same issue on master branch:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/e11d848c-fc59-11e8-8512-52540065bddc
